def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd; import numpy as np; import ta
    
    # Calculate Bollinger Bands
    df['bb_upper'] = ta.volatility.bollinger_hband(df['close'], window=params['bb_window'], window_dev=params['bb_dev'])
    df['bb_lower'] = ta.volatility.bollinger_lband(df['close'], window=params['bb_window'], window_dev=params['bb_dev'])
    
    # Calculate Keltner Channel
    df['kc_upper'] = ta.volatility.keltner_channel_hband(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=params['kc_window'], window_atr=params['kc_atr_window'], fillna=False)
    df['kc_lower'] = ta.volatility.keltner_channel_lband(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=params['kc_window'], window_atr=params['kc_atr_window'], fillna=False)
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Long when price breaks above both BB upper and KC upper (volatility expansion)
    signals[(df['close'] > df['bb_upper']) & (df['close'] > df['kc_upper'])] = 1
    
    # Short when price breaks below both BB lower and KC lower (volatility expansion)
    signals[(df['close'] < df['bb_lower']) & (df['close'] < df['kc_lower'])] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: GBP/JPY exhibits strong volatility expansion patterns where simultaneous breakouts from Bollinger Bands and Keltner Channels indicate sustained directional moves.
